idea-plugin.name: Jujutsu VCS Integration
idea-plugin.description:
Native IntelliJ integration for Jujutsu (jj), a Git-compatible version control system with a fundamentally different approach to working with code.

Why Jujutsu?

Jujutsu treats your working copy as a commit—always. No staging area, no "dirty" state. Just describe what you're working on and keep coding. When you're ready to start something new, create a new change. It's version control that works the way you think.

Key Features

  • Describe-First Workflow — The Working Copy tool window (left side) lets you describe your current work and create new changes with one click. No more "WIP" commits.
  • Custom Log View — Visual commit graph with inline change IDs, descriptions, bookmarks, and author info. Filter by text, branch, author, or date range. Multi-select changes to see a combined diff or stacked metadata. Copy change IDs and descriptions from the context menu.
  • Change Operations — Edit, abandon, describe, rebase, squash, and split changes directly from the log. Right-click context menus for all common operations.
  • Rebase — Full rebase dialog with source mode selection (-r/-s/-b), visual destination picker with commit graph, and a live post-rebase graph preview showing exactly where commits will land.
  • Squash & Split — Squash changes into parent or any destination. Split into two with a dual file-tree UI that lets you write descriptions for both halves. Right-click files in the working copy, project view, or editor to squash or split with those files pre-selected.
  • Restore Files — Restore any file to its parent revision from the editor, project view, working copy panel, or commit details.
  • Compare & Diff — Compare any file against any revision or bookmark using a visual revision picker. Show diffs of working copy changes or historical commits. Compare historical versions against local working copy.
  • Git Remotes — Fetch and push to Git remotes without leaving the IDE. Push dialog with remote selector and bookmark scope options. Dry-run detection warns before non-fast-forward bookmark moves; sideways pushes require explicit confirmation.
  • File History & Annotations — Full file history with diff viewer and Show History for Selection (line-range history). Line-by-line blame annotations showing who changed what and when.
  • Open in GitHub / GitLab — Right-click any commit, file-history entry, or diff to jump to it on the remote, with line-number fragments for editor selections.
  • Bookmark Management — Create, delete, rename, and move bookmarks from the log. Track and untrack remote bookmarks; tracked vs. untracked are visually distinct.
  • Clone & First-Run Setup — Clone Git repos with jj from File > New > Project from Version Control, with colocate option. Plugin detects your jj install and walks you through user.name / user.email setup.
  • Configurable Log Revsets — Filter the log with any custom revset expression. A Test button validates the expression against each repo and shows how many changes match before you save.
  • Multi-Repository Support — Work with multiple JJ repositories in a single project. Unified log view with per-repo filtering, and per-repository overrides for identity, log limit, and revset.
  • Real-Time Status — Auto-refresh keeps the UI in sync as you edit files. See modified, added, and deleted files instantly.

For JJ Users

If you're already using JJ from the command line, this plugin brings that workflow into your IDE. All the power of jj describe, jj new, jj edit, jj abandon, and jj rebase—with visual feedback and keyboard shortcuts.

Getting Started

  1. Install Jujutsu if you haven't already
  2. Open a project with a .jj directory (or initialize one with VCS → Create JJ Repository)
  3. The Working Copy panel appears on the left; the Jujutsu log appears in the Version Control tool window

Roadmap

Coming soon: hunk-level squash/split with destination picker, branches panel and bookmark toolbar widget, conflict resolution UI, operation log with undo/redo, remote management, and forge (PR/MR) integration. See the full roadmap.

GitHub · Issues
